article_title: Google Ends Gemini CLI for Free Users After 6,000 Community Contributions, Replacing Open Source Tool With Closed-Source Antigravity CLI
summary: Well-sourced News article covering Google's Gemini CLI shutdown with all quotes and claims verified; automated warnings are domain allowlist gaps only.

content_preview.title: Google Ends Gemini CLI for Free Users After 6,000 Community Contributions, Replacing Open Source Tool With Closed-Source Antigravity CLI
content_preview.summary: Google will shut down free access to the Apache 2.0-licensed Gemini CLI on June 18, replacing it with a closed-source Go rewrite that enterprise subscribers alone will inherit.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Google announced on May 19, 2026 that Gemini CLI — the open source AI coding terminal it released under the Apache 2.0 license in June 2025 — will stop serving requests for free and non-enterprise users on June 18, 2026. The tool attracted more than 6,000 merged pull requests from external contributors and accumulated over 100,000 GitHub stars before Google unveiled its replacement: Antigravity CLI, a closed-source Go rewrite with no publicly available source code, according to [FOS...

recommendations: Consider adding fossforce.com and theregister.com to the source allowlist

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured, thorough News article on Google's Gemini CLI shutdown. Covers the timeline, the open-source community reaction, the nature of the replacement (Antigravity CLI), and provides editorial analysis of the pattern. Good use of direct quotes from community members.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 6 sources verified from disk snapshots. source-0.html.gz (FOSS Force): confirms Lyalin/Mullen quote 'We can serve you best by pouring our energy into a single product built for today's multi-agent reality' — article truncates at 'single product' without ellipsis, which is acceptable editorial practice that doesn't distort meaning. Confirms June 18 shutdown date and enterprise exemption. source-1.html.gz (The Register): confirms Antigravity CLI GitHub repo contains only changelog/readme/GIF, no source code; confirms '1:1 feature parity' quote; confirms enterprise exemption details. source-2.html.gz (TechTimes): confirms Andrea Alberti had a 27-commit pull request merged same day as announcement; confirms secretnarwhal and @lingyaochu quotes verbatim. source-3.html.gz (TechTimes): confirms 31 thumbs-down as top reaction; confirms @lingyaochu quote; confirms Killed by Google listing. source-4.html.gz (ByteIota): confirms Antigravity CLI is Go rewrite (not fork), 5-hour quota refresh with weekly ceiling, four-week migration window for Figma/Shopify/Stripe. source-5.html.gz (Shopifreaks): confirms four-week migration window for launch partners including Figma, Shopify, Stripe.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified. One minor note: the article says 'Andrea Alberti, a contributor with 27 merged commits' while sources describe 'a 27-commit pull request' — this is a minor framing difference (27 commits in one PR vs 27 merged commits) that does not constitute a factual error. All key claims about quota model, enterprise exemption, GitHub repo contents, and launch partners confirmed.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. The Analysis section accurately characterizes the Apache 2.0 license situation without editorializing. Community reaction quotes are properly attributed.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Gemini CLI shutdown coverage found in the archive. Original topic.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality News article. All integrity checks pass, all sources verified. Overriding automated APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS (triggered by domain allowlist gaps) to APPROVE.
